title: Catamaran Rental in Puerto Banus: Costs & Guide for 2026
html:
Puerto Banus in Marbella is the Costa del Sol's most glamorous marina, and catamaran rental here offers a spacious, stable way to explore the Mediterranean. On BoatHire24, you can find crewed catamarans from around EUR 400 for a half-day trip, departing from the port's main dock.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

A half-day catamaran charter in Puerto Banus typically costs between EUR 400 and EUR 800, while a full day ranges from EUR 800 to EUR 1,500. Prices vary by boat size, season, and whether you want a skipper or a full crew. For a premium Lagoon or Leopard catamaran with a skipper, you can expect to pay around EUR 1,200 for a full day. To compare prices and availability, you can browse boats for your dates on the platform.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most catamaran rentals depart from the main Puerto Banus marina, which has direct access to the Mediterranean. From there, you can cruise along the coast to Marbella's iconic beaches, such as Playa de la Fontanilla or Playa de Cabopino. A popular sailing route goes westward to the exclusive port of Sotogrande, about 25 nautical miles away, or east to the chic resort of Estepona. For a shorter trip, head to the nearby Cabo Pino inlet or anchor off the dunes of Artola, a natural park perfect for swimming.

Which boat to rent

  1. Lagoon 40 catamaran, ideal for a family day trip; sleeps up to 8 guests; from EUR 600 half-day.
  2. Leopard 53 catamaran, spacious for larger groups or parties; capacity 12 guests; from EUR 1,200 full day. Check this catamaran for details on the Zambezia model.
  3. Fountaine Pajot Astrea 42, a good balance of comfort and performance; up to 10 guests; from EUR 750 half-day.
  4. Nautitech 46 Open, a sporty catamaran with a flybridge; suitable for 10 guests; from EUR 900 half-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

For crewed catamaran rentals, no licence is required as the captain handles navigation; for bareboat rentals, you need a Spanish Patron de Embarcaciones de Recreo or equivalent international certificate. Most visitors opt for a skippered charter, which also includes a host if you book a full-day trip. Always confirm with the operator on the exact requirements, as rules can vary for non-EU licence holders.

Tip: book a morning departure in July or August to avoid the strong Levante wind that often picks up by early afternoon.

Costs at a glance

  • Price band: EUR 400, 1,500 per half/full day
  • Peak months: June through September
  • Typical trip length: 4 or 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 8, 12, depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about catamaran rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a catamaran for a party or event?

Yes, many catamarans in Puerto Banus are available for celebrations, with catering and sound systems optional at an extra cost.

What is included in a catamaran charter?

A typical crewed charter includes a skipper, fuel, safety equipment, and often a host; food and drinks are usually extra unless you choose an all-inclusive package.

Can I swim or snorkel from a catamaran?

Absolutely, most trips include stops in secluded coves along the Costa del Sol, such as the waters near Cabo Pino or Marbella's beach clubs.

How far in advance should I book for August 2026?

Peak season catamarans can sell out weeks ahead, so booking at least one month in advance is recommended.

To explore current availability, browse the listings on BoatHire24. If you own a catamaran in this area, you can also list it on the platform to reach thousands of travellers. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
